title: Tool result type uses (String, bool) primitive pair instead of Result<T, E>
description:
## Severity
Critical

## Dimension
Error Orchestration / Decoupling / SOLID-I (ISP)

## Location
src/tools/file/read.rs:40, src/tools/file/write.rs:9, src/tools/file/edit.rs:9, src/tools/file/notebook.rs:49, src/tools/file/list.rs:7, src/tools/file/mod.rs:188, src/tools/plan_mode.rs:8, src/tools/worktree.rs:59, src/tools/cron.rs:142, src/tools/todo.rs:76 (every public executor)

## Violation
Every tool executor in this subsystem returns `(String, bool)` where `bool` means is_error. Quoting `read_text_file` (read.rs:273):

```rust
pub fn read_text_file(path: &str, args: &HashMap<String, Value>) -> (String, bool) {
    ...
    Err(e) => (format!("Failed to read file '{path}': {e}"), true),
```

This is a textbook boolean-flag-return anti-pattern (Section 3.3 — "Reject boolean flag arguments immediately"). It throws away typed error information, conflates the success and error channels in one type, prevents `?` propagation, forces every caller to remember which slot is which, and obstructs structured logging (no error kind, source, or context). Callers cannot pattern-match on error variants; they can only grep the message string.

## Mandated refactor
Introduce a typed `ToolError` enum (with `thiserror`) and change every executor signature to `Result<ToolOutput, ToolError>` where `ToolOutput` is a struct carrying `payload: String` plus typed metadata (warnings, diff blocks). Replace the `(message, true)` returns with `Err(ToolError::MissingArg("path"))`, `Err(ToolError::PathTraversal(p))`, `Err(ToolError::ReadBeforeEditViolation)`, etc. Have a single boundary adapter convert `Result<ToolOutput, ToolError>` into the wire format at the dispatch layer, not at every executor.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
